steps.title: Add 7 to both sides to isolate the product
steps.content:
The equation $(d-30)(d+30)-7=-7$ has $-7$ on both sides. Adding $7$ to both sides eliminates these constants:
$$(d-30)(d+30) = 0$$

steps.title: Apply the zero product property
steps.content:
A product equals zero if and only if at least one factor equals zero. Therefore:
$$d - 30 = 0 \quad \text{or} \quad d + 30 = 0$$

steps.title: Solve each factor for d
steps.content:
From the first equation: $d = 30$
From the second equation: $d = -30$

Both values satisfy the original equation. One solution is $d = 30$ — 30 is correct.
